title: Lifecycle tracking for machine components
abstract: A system (22) for tracking components (38) of a machine (10) and a method for tracking the lifecycle of a component for a machine is provided. The system may have an electronic chip (30) having a serial number (32), wherein the electronic chip is integrated within a component of the machine. The system may also have a communication module (24) operatively connected to the electronic chip via a communication bus (28) and a transmitter module (26) operatively connected to the communication module. The communication module may read the serial number of the electronic chip and the transmitter module transmits the serial number to a central database (42), causing the central database to associate the serial number and the component to the machine.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A system (22) for tracking components of a machine (10), comprising: an electronic chip (30) having a serial number (32), wherein the electronic chip is integrated within a component (38) of the machine; a communication module (24) operatively connected to the electronic chip via a communication bus (28); and a transmitter module (26) operatively connected to the communi cati on modul e ; wherein the communication module reads the serial number of the electronic chip and the transmitter module transmits the serial number to a central database (42), causing the central database to associate the serial number and the component to the machine. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the component of the machine is at least one of a fuel filter, an oil filter, an air filter, a hydraulic fluid filter, a transmission fluid filter, a sensor, a work implement, a pump, a turbocharger, a transmission, an engine head, and a fuel injector. 3. The system of claim 1, further comprising a plurality of electronic chips each having a serial number, wherein the electronic chips are each integrated with a different component of the machine and the communication module is operatively connected to the plurality of electronic chips via the communication bus. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein data is saved on the electronic chip and the communication module reads the data and transmits the 

 data to the central database, the data includes at least one of manufacturing data, sales data, and end user data. 5. The system of claim 4, wherein: the manufacturing data for the component includes at least one of a part number, a date of manufacture, a manufacturer name, and a location of manufacture; the sales data for the component includes at least one of a dealer name, an end user name, or a date of sale; and the end user data for the component includes at least one of a date of installation, a service technician identifier, or a machine identifier for the machine. 6. A method for tracking the lifecycle of a component for a machine, the method comprising: assigning a serial number to the component by integrating an electronic chip having the serial number into the component; associating manufacturing data of the component to the serial number as part of the manufacturing process for the component; associating end user data of the component to the serial number as part of the installation process for the component; and collecting the manufacturing data and the end user data of the component in a central database, causing the central database to associate the serial number, the manufacturing data, and the end user data with the machine. 7. The method of claim 6, further including associating sales data of the component to the serial number as part of the sale transaction for the component, wherein the manufacturing data, the sales data, and the end user data of the component is combined to form lifecycle data for the component. 8. The method of claim 7, further comprising selectively sharing the lifecycle data with at least one of the manufacturer, the dealer, and the end user of the component. 9. The method of claim 7, further comprising: determining and tracking a maintenance schedule for the machine based on the lifecycle data; and using the lifecycle data of the component to identify if the component is a counterfeit. 10. The method of claim 7, wherein: associating the manufacturing data of the component to the serial number includes at least one of writing the manufacturing data to the electronic chip and transmitting the manufacturing data along with the serial number to the central database, which saves the manufacturing data with the serial number; associating the sales data of the component to the serial number includes at least one of writing the sales data to the electronic chip or transmitting the sales data along with the serial number to the central database, which saves the sales data with the serial number; and associating the end user data of the component to the serial number includes at least one of writing the end user data to the electronic chip or transmitting the end user data along with the serial number to the central database, which saves the end user data with the serial number.
